scope: private-beta runtime health, durable progress, resilience, account data lifecycle, operations-control, and deterministic fault-campaign implementation evidence

browserVerification.console: no output on the final production-built account data page

visualEvidence.alt: Desktop ReproForge case page showing the tenant-scoped identity boundary while Auth0 is not configured.
Mobile ReproForge case page showing the tenant-scoped identity boundary without horizontal overflow.
Desktop ReproForge ChatGPT widget preview showing verified proof, evidence, runs, and bundle state.
Mobile ReproForge ChatGPT widget preview showing verified proof without horizontal overflow.
Desktop ReproForge account data page showing export, explicit deletion confirmation, and retention disclosures in the fail-closed identity setup state.
Mobile ReproForge account data page showing responsive export, deletion, and retention controls without horizontal overflow.

contractEvidence.provenance: schema-validated synthetic local aggregate; not a hosted operations snapshot
fixed-seed synthetic local resilience campaign summary; not a hosted load or fault-injection result

redGreenFindings.red: Hosted runner health always returned RUNNER_NOT_CONFIGURED even though isolated execution was composed.
redGreenFindings.green: A bounded real Node 24 sandbox probe now proves deny-all execution, validates a marker, stops the sandbox, and exposes only stable health codes.

redGreenFindings.red: A hosted preview could report durable providers ready while Auth0, OAuth resource, or GitHub App configuration was absent.
redGreenFindings.green: Hosted readiness now validates all product configuration and canonical origins before any provider probe runs.

redGreenFindings.red: REST, MCP, widget, and web surfaces had independent or implicit progress representations.
redGreenFindings.green: All four surfaces now consume one strict durable progress projection, covered by 500 generated schedules and an executable BDD journey.

redGreenFindings.red: Runner degradation, expired work, and cleanup failure lacked one fail-closed admission and operator recovery path.
redGreenFindings.green: New starts are denied while reads remain available; worker loss and cancellation follow legal durable transitions; exact quarantined sandbox or snapshot cleanup is provider-deleted before a sanitized resolution audit is appended.

redGreenFindings.red: Tenant archives were internal structures and users had no authenticated export or deletion controls.
redGreenFindings.green: A strict portable archive verifies its manifest and object digests, authenticated account routes enforce quota/idempotency/origin/confirmation boundaries, and provider-first deletion suspends and cancels work before purging tenant data.

redGreenFindings.red: Private-beta operations had health primitives but no aggregate dashboard, owned alert thresholds, feature kill switches, or executable rollback compatibility contract.
redGreenFindings.green: A schema-closed identifier-free dashboard evaluates eight owned alerts, three fail-closed switches deny only new repository work, and an executable policy pins the additive schema window and application-only rollback procedure.

redGreenFindings.red: Failure tests were individually useful but lacked one exact, repeatable campaign registry covering every required RF-8406 boundary.
redGreenFindings.green: A schema-closed eight-category registry now drives an exact gate across real durable adapters, fixed-seed property schedules, 128-way start/read load, restart reconstruction, duplicate delivery, provider degradation, worker loss, queue lag, storage failure, and sandbox failure.

deferred: live Auth0 tenant and OAuth linking
live GitHub App installation and repository selection
hosted RF-8403 recovery and quarantine exercise
hosted RF-8404 retention, deletion, and backup/restore exercise
hosted RF-8405 alert delivery, deployment, and rollback rehearsal
hosted RF-8406 load and failure-injection campaign
sanitization: Evidence contains only the trusted synthetic fixture, a synthetic inaccessible case identifier, the signed-out/unconfigured account-data state, an identifier-free synthetic operations aggregate, and fixed-seed synthetic resilience counts; no credentials, sessions, private repository names, customer data, export bytes, deletion identifiers, or provider resource identifiers are committed.
